BELDING INDIA: ACQUIRES 100% STAKE IN EVOLVE IT SOLUTIONS 🚀🛡️

• 🤝 Belding India has acquired a 100% equity stake in Evolve IT Solutions Pvt. Ltd., strengthening its presence in advanced manufacturing and security technologies. 

• 🛡️ Evolve specializes in advanced X-ray inspection and security screening solutions, including baggage scanners, cargo inspection systems, vehicle scanners, under-vehicle surveillance systems and dual-view X-ray systems, serving airports, ports, defence, metro rail and other critical infrastructure. 

• ⚙️ The acquisition expands Belding's capabilities in AI-enabled imaging, industrial electronics, precision engineering and mission-critical security technologies, creating opportunities in both domestic and international markets. 

• 🇮🇳 The company said the acquisition supports its strategy to build an indigenous engineering and technology platform, while promoting Make in India, import substitution and long-term growth. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition broadens Belding's technology portfolio, strengthens its defence and security offerings, and enhances long-term growth prospects.)

HARIOM PIPE: CRISIL REVISES RATING OUTLOOK TO 'STABLE' 📈🏦

• ⭐ CRISIL Ratings has revised the outlook on Hariom Pipe Industries' long-term bank facilities from 'CRISIL A-/Watch Developing' to 'CRISIL A-/Stable'. 

• ✅ The short-term non-fund based rating has also been revised from 'CRISIL A2+/Watch Developing' to 'CRISIL A2+', with the 'Watch Developing' status removed. 

• 🏦 The revised ratings apply to the company's fund-based long-term and non-fund based short-term banking facilities. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Removal of the 'Watch Developing' status and assignment of a stable outlook reflect improved credit profile and enhance the company's credibility with lenders.)

Key takeaways from Nandan Kulkarni interview on the potential impact of US pharma tariffs.

While the market is focused on tariff headlines, Nandan argues that the bigger story is how tariffs could reshape the economics of the US generic drug industry.

Here are the insights that stood out to us 

1. The generic pricing model may finally reset
 - According to Nandan, US generic drug prices have been under pressure for years. If manufacturing shifts back to the US, the current pricing simply won't support the economics.
 - Higher labour costs, operating expenses, FDA site transfers, and fresh capital investment all require higher Average Selling Prices (ASPs) to make domestic manufacturing viable.
 - The industry may be entering a structural pricing reset rather than facing just another policy headwind.

2. Drug shortages could become a major catalyst
- One interesting point he highlighted is that shortages in several acute drug categories have already started appearing in the US.
 - Any attempt to accelerate onshoring could tighten supply further before new capacity comes online.
 - That potentially creates:
 • Better pricing
 • Improved profitability
 • Stronger negotiating power for reliable manufacturers
Sometimes the second-order effects matter more than the headline itself.

3. Existing US manufacturing becomes a competitive advantage
Not every company starts from the same position.
Nandan believes companies that already have manufacturing operations in the US—such as Sun Pharma, Lupin and Zydus Lifesciences—could adapt much faster if localisation gathers pace. Rather than building entirely new facilities, these companies may be able to expand existing sites and execute product transfers more efficiently.

4. The market may be underestimating the timeline
A key observation was that investors shouldn't wait for tariffs to be formally implemented.
The next 12–18 months could already see:
 • Pricing negotiations with PBMs
 • Strategic inventory build-up
 • Manufacturing transfer planning
 • Incremental US capex decisions
Markets often discount these developments well before policy becomes effective.

5. Execution—not tariffs—will separate winners
 - An important distinction Nandan made was between simple oral generics and complex biologics.
 - Traditional tablets and capsules are relatively easier to relocate.
 - Biosimilars and other complex products involve significantly longer technology transfers and regulatory approvals, making the transition far more challenging.

6. Better pricing could fund future growth
 - One concern investors have is whether companies will need massive balance sheet investments.
 - Nandan's argument is interesting: if generic prices improve because of tighter supply and higher manufacturing costs, part of the incremental profitability itself could finance future US manufacturing investments.
 - That changes the capital allocation equation.

7. The long-term opportunity extends beyond generics
Perhaps the most important takeaway was his long-term view.
Rather than remaining low-cost manufacturers, Indian pharma companies could accelerate investments in:
 - Complex generics
 - 505(b)(2) products
 - Specialty pharmaceuticals
 - Orphan drugs
 - Peptides
 - Innovation-led portfolios
If that transition plays out, tariffs may ultimately become a catalyst for higher-value pharmaceutical businesses rather than simply a near-term disruption.

Our takeaway
The discussion shifts the focus away from "Will tariffs hurt Indian pharma?" to a more important question:
Could tariffs accelerate a healthier industry structure with better pricing, stronger margins and greater innovation?

HINDUSTAN ZINC: DISCLOSES PROMOTER GROUP'S $2.25 BN FACILITY AGREEMENT 📑

• 🏦 Hindustan Zinc informed exchanges about a $2.25 billion Facility Agreement entered into by Twin Star Holdings, along with Vedanta group entities

• 📌 HZL is not a party to the agreement, but certain identified clauses may become applicable to HZL as a Vedanta group company after facility utilisation

• ⚖️ Agreement contains customary lender protections and covenants, including restrictions on certain actions such as creation of security, mergers, asset transfers and specific transactions, subject to agreed carve-outs

• 🚫 Company clarified there is no direct impact on HZL's management or control, and no quantified liability has been imposed on HZL

• ℹ️ Disclosure has been made under SEBI Regulation 30A due to the promoter group's financing arrangement

🟡 Impact: Neutral (This is a regulatory disclosure relating to promoter group financing. HZL is not a borrower or guarantor, and the company has stated there is no direct impact on its management or control.)

Infosys | Q1 FY27 Results

Financial Highlights
- Revenue ₹48,211 crore (+14% YoY, +4% QoQ).
- EBIT ₹10,163 crore (+30% YoY, +17% QoQ).
- EBIT Margin 21.1% (vs 20.8% YoY).
- PAT ₹7,769 crore (+12% YoY, -9% QoQ).
- CC Revenue Growth +1% QoQ.
- Dollar Revenue US$5.08 billion (+1% QoQ).

Deal Wins & Business Momentum
- TCV increased to US$3.6 billion (+13% QoQ).
- Strong large deal wins across segments.
- AI momentum translating into revenue.
- Continued market share gains.
- Infosys Topaz driving enterprise AI adoption.

Management Commentary
- AI demand gaining traction.
- Strategic partnerships with leading AI companies.
- Focus on employee AI reskilling.
- Continued investments in AI, talent and platforms.
- Improving productivity and operating leverage.

Guidance
- FY27 revenue growth guidance revised to 1.5%–3.0%.
- Earlier guidance was 1.5%–3.5%.
- Reflects a more cautious growth outlook.

Leadership Transition
- Ashiss Kumar Dash appointed CEO & MD-designate.
- To succeed Salil Parekh from 1 April 2027.
- Over 31 years with Infosys.
- Expected to lead the company's next AI-led growth phase.

Impact
- Neutral.
- Strong execution and deal pipeline.
- AI business continues to strengthen.
- Guidance cut may weigh on near-term sentiment.
- Leadership transition appears well planned.

Key Takeaway
- Infosys delivered healthy Q1 FY27 growth with stable margins, robust deal wins and increasing AI-led revenue momentum. However, the reduction in FY27 revenue guidance indicates a more cautious demand environment. While near-term sentiment may remain muted, the strong order book, continued AI investments and a well-planned leadership transition position the company well for long-term growth.

INFOSYS Q1 : Board approved its intention to appoint Ashiss Kumar Dash as the Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director of the Company with effect from April 1, 2027, upon fulfilment of all statutory requirements.

Dash is currently the global head of a diverse business portfolio comprising multiple industry verticals and is planned to succeed Salil Parekh as Managing Director & Chief Executive Officer on April 1, 2027, following the completion of Salil's second term, after over 9 years.

The Board believes this combination of strategic and commercial acumen together with his technology delivery experience, uniquely positions him to lead Infosys through its next phase of Al-led transformation, while building on the Company's enduring strengths.

Jubilant Ingrevia | Q1 FY27 Results

Financial Highlights
- Revenue ₹1,300 crore (+25% YoY, +10% QoQ).
- EBITDA ₹209 crore (+36% YoY, +22% QoQ).
- EBITDA Margin 16% (vs 15% last year).
- PAT ₹106 crore (+41% YoY, +22% QoQ).
- EPS ₹6.7 (+41% YoY, +22% QoQ).

Business Performance
- Strong performance across all business segments.
- Broad-based revenue and profit growth.
- Healthy margin expansion.
- Better operational execution.

Key Monitorables
- Progress of the US$300 million agrochemical project.
- Customer execution and project timelines.
- Sustainability of volume growth.
- Contribution from pricing versus volumes.
- Management commentary on demand outlook.

Long-Term Growth Drivers
- Execution of the Pinnacle Strategy.
- Higher contribution from Specialty Chemicals.
- Expansion of CDMO business.
- Improving business mix towards value-added segments.
- Potential for stronger long-term profitability.

Impact
- Positive.
- Strong quarterly performance.
- Margin improvement continues.
- Execution remains the key value driver.
- Management commentary will determine earnings sustainability.

Key Takeaway
- Jubilant Ingrevia delivered a strong Q1 FY27 with healthy growth in revenue, profitability and margins across all business segments. While the quarterly performance is encouraging, the long-term investment case continues to depend on successful execution of the Pinnacle Strategy, progress on the US$300 million agrochemical project and the company's ability to increase the share of higher-margin Specialty Chemicals and CDMO businesses.

BAJAJ FINANCE: RAISES ₹1,152.64 CRORE VIA PRIVATE PLACEMENT OF NCDs 💰📑

• 💵 Bajaj Finance has allotted 1,14,076 Secured Redeemable Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) of ₹1 lakh face value each, raising ₹1,152.64 crore through a private placement. 

• 📅 The NCDs were allotted on July 23, 2026, carry a coupon rate of 7.93% per annum, and will mature on June 12, 2029. Interest will be paid annually, with the first coupon due on June 12, 2027. 

• 🏛️ The debentures are proposed to be listed on the Wholesale Debt Market Segment of BSE. 

• 🛡️ The NCDs are secured by a first pari-passu charge on book debts/loan receivables, with a minimum 1x security cover, and will be redeemed on maturity.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The successful debt raise strengthens Bajaj Finance's funding base, supporting future lending growth while maintaining a diversified liability profile.)

PRECISION WIRES: SILVASSA PLANTS PARTIALLY IMPACTED BY FLOODING 🌧️🏭

• 🌧️ Precision Wires said operations at its Silvassa plants have been partially impacted due to flooding and waterlogging caused by unprecedented heavy rainfall since July 21, 2026. Around 740 mm of rain was recorded in the last 24 hours, following 333 mm during the previous day. 

• 🔍 The company is assessing the extent of production loss and property damage, and the financial impact is yet to be quantified. 

• 🛡️ Precision Wires confirmed that it is adequately insured under an Industrial All Risk (IAR) insurance policy. 

• 🔄 The company expects operations to gradually normalize over the next few days, barring any unforeseen developments, while ensuring the safety of employees, equipment and materials.  

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Operations have been temporarily disrupted, but the impact is still being assessed, the assets are insured and the company expects a gradual normalization.)

reports.text:
VISHAL MEGA MART: TO CAP FOREIGN OWNERSHIP AT 49.99% 🇮🇳📊

• 🇮🇳 The Board has approved a proposal to cap aggregate foreign ownership at 49.99% of the company's total equity instruments (on a fully diluted basis), subject to shareholders' approval. The company will issue the required notice in due course.

• 🏢 The proposal has been made because one of the company's wholly owned subsidiaries is required to remain an Indian-owned and controlled company (IOCC) under applicable FEMA regulations.

• 📜 The company clarified that while it is currently Indian-owned and controlled, the cap is being introduced in line with SEBI's framework that allows listed companies to restrict aggregate FPI/non-resident investment with Board and shareholder approval.

• 👩‍💼 The Board also re-appointed Ms. Neha Bansal as a Non-Executive Independent Director for a second term of one year, from September 23, 2026 to September 22, 2027, subject to shareholders' approval.

🟢 Impact: Positive (The foreign ownership cap helps preserve the company's Indian-owned and controlled status, ensuring regulatory compliance and protecting operations of its subsidiary, while maintaining business continuity.)

reports.text:
PTC Industries has received a design & development order from ARDE (DRDO) for a Titanium Cradle used in the Indian Light Weight Tank programme.

Moving up the value chain! 

While the order value hasn't been disclosed due to the strategic nature of the project, the announcement is noteworthy for another reason.

Unlike conventional manufacturing contracts, this assignment requires PTC to design, develop, validate and manufacture a mission-critical titanium component.

This indicates a shift from executing customer-provided designs to participating earlier in the product development cycle, where engineering capability, material expertise and validation become equally important.

Titanium is increasingly being used in defence applications because of its high strength-to-weight ratio, corrosion resistance and durability, making it suitable for lightweight platforms such as the Indian Light Weight Tank.

The execution timeline for the project is 2.5 years, and further developments would depend on successful design validation and subsequent programme requirements.

PTC INDUSTRIES: BAGS DRDO DESIGN & DEVELOPMENT ORDER FOR INDIAN LIGHT WEIGHT TANK 🛡️🇮🇳

• 🛰️ Received a design & development order from ARDE, DRDO for a Titanium Cradle for the 105mm Indian Light Weight Tank

• 🚀 Marks PTC Industries' first fit-for-purpose design order, expanding beyond conventional build-to-print manufacturing

• ⚙️ Titanium Cradle is a mission-critical defence component supporting the tank's main gun barrel, breech and recoil mechanism

• 🏭 Strengthens PTC ONE™ ("From Melt to Mission") by adding indigenous design-led capability for advanced defence components

• 🇮🇳 Opens opportunities in lightweight titanium components for tanks, artillery systems and other next-generation defence platforms

🟢 Impact: Positive (Enhances PTC Industries' defence capabilities, moves up the value chain into design-led manufacturing and strengthens long-term growth opportunities.)

JSW Infrastructure

Q1 Highlights
- Revenue rose 18% YoY to ₹1,445 crore.
- EBITDA increased 16% YoY to ₹674 crore.
- Port revenue stood at ₹1,128 crore.

Guidance & Outlook
- Murbe Port construction to begin by Dec–Jan.
- Slurry pipeline commissioning by March 2027.

- Rail fleet target 250 rakes.

- FY28 EBITDA guidance ₹5,000 crore.

---

Adani Energy Solutions

Q1 Highlights
- Energy Solutions revenue ₹600 crore.
- Smart meter revenue ₹161 crore.
- Energy handled 13,181 MUs.
- Transmission network reached 28,000 ckm.
- Installed 1.34 crore smart meters.

Guidance & Outlook

- Focus on annuity-based energy contracts.

- Energy Solutions target 7.5 GW by FY31.
- Sees ₹1 lakh crore+ annual bidding opportunity.

---

Mastek

Q1 Highlights
- Revenue rose 7.7% YoY to ₹985 crore.
- EBITDA margin stood at 15.4%.
- PAT reached ₹106 crore.
- Order backlog grew 25% YoY to US$310 million.
- Net cash improved by ₹200 crore.
- Won US$25 million AI transformation deal.
- Added 13 new customers.
- Attrition improved to 16.4%.

Guidance & Outlook
- Closed 40 AI-led projects in Q1.
- AI transformation remains key focus.
- FY27 expected to outperform FY26.
- Q2 margins may face temporary pressure.
- Strong AI and North America pipeline.
- Healthy backlog supports growth visibility.

---

E2E Networks

Guidance & Outlook
- No specific MRR guidance.
- 75.2% EBITDA margin seen sustainable.
- Capacity expansion to continue aggressively.
- New B200 GPUs to be deployed.
- Expansion into Blackwell and Vera Rubin.
- Longer contracts improving revenue visibility.
- Positioned for AI infrastructure supercycle.

---

Dr. Reddy's Laboratories

Guidance & Outlook
- EBITDA margin around 20% next quarter.
- Semaglutide supplies to resume in November.
- Plans to supply 6–7 million pens.
- R&D spend guided at 7–8% of revenue.
- FY27 tax rate 24–25%.
- FY27 capex around ₹1,800 crore.
- Biologics profitability expected after abatacept launch.
- US approval targeted by December 2026.
- Targeting 27 US product launches this year.

---

Nippon Life India AMC

Guidance & Outlook
- Other expenses to rise 18–20%.
- Employee costs to remain broadly stable.
- Overall yield may decline 1–2 bps.
- FY27 ESOP cost around ₹60 crore.
- Ready for differentiated SIF launches.
- DWS JV expected to attract European capital.

Key Takeaway
- JSW Infrastructure, Adani Energy Solutions, Mastek and E2E Networks maintained strong long-term growth outlooks backed by capacity expansion and robust order pipelines. Dr. Reddy's expects earnings recovery with semaglutide resumption, while Nippon Life AMC continues investing for long-term AUM growth despite near-term cost pressures.

Daily live Concall  highlights update 
@Concalls3

US Student Visa | Policy Update

Key Changes
- New rule effective September 15, 2026.
- "Duration of Status (D/S)" system ends.
- New entries capped at four years.
- Extensions require separate USCIS approval.
- Grace period reduced to 30 days.

For Current Students
- Students outside the US should return before Sept. 15.
- Those in the US by Sept. 15 can continue studies/OPT.
- Overall stay capped at four years.
- Stay cannot extend beyond November 2030.

Impact
- Affects F-1 and J-1 visa holders.
- Indian students among the most impacted.
- Universities advising early return.

Key Takeaway
- The proposed US student visa rule changes significantly alter stay and extension rules for F-1 and J-1 students. Eligible students currently outside the US may benefit from returning before September 15, 2026, while future entrants will face stricter time limits and approval requirements.

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹1,250 (Raised).

**Q1 Assessment**
- Profit beat estimates.
- Growth trends improving.
- Asset quality strengthened.
- Core ROA improved.
- Lower opex and credit costs supported earnings.

**Outlook**
- Improvement expected over next 2–3 years.
- Driven by new management team.
- Expects 1.1% ROA by FY29.

**Valuation**
- Values stock at 1.3x FY27 adjusted P/B.
- 1.5% ROA seen as rerating trigger.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on IndusInd Bank, citing improving earnings quality, strengthening asset quality, and a gradual ROA recovery, with sustained profitability improvement seen as the key catalyst for valuation rerating.**
Jefferies maintains 'Buy' ratings on Eternal, hikes target price to Rs 415.

Blinkit | Business Update

Expansion
- Targets smaller towns.
- Operates 2,400+ dark stores.
- Presence in 300+ cities.

Store Economics
- Tier-2 break-even at ~800 orders/day.
- Metros need ~1,300 orders/day.
- Lower costs improve profitability.
- Average 1,600 orders/store/day.

Product Expansion
- ~80,000 SKUs in Delhi-NCR.
- ~50,000 SKUs in next seven cities.
- ~20,000 SKUs in smaller towns.

Key Takeaway
- Blinkit's expansion into smaller towns is backed by attractive unit economics, strong order density, and a wider product assortment, reinforcing the scalability of quick commerce beyond metro markets.

• 🛡️ Subam Papers has proposed to provide a ₹40 crore corporate guarantee in favour of YES Bank for credit facilities extended to its wholly owned subsidiary, Subam Paper and Boards Pvt. Ltd.

GODREJ CONSUMER: INVESTS ₹200 CRORE IN GODREJ PET CARE VIA RIGHTS ISSUE 🐶💰

• 🏢 Godrej Consumer Products has invested ₹200 crore in its wholly owned subsidiary, Godrej Pet Care Ltd., by subscribing to a rights issue to fund the subsidiary's business operations, growth plans and capital requirements. 

• 🐾 The investment aims to strengthen GCPL's presence in the fast-growing pet care category, which the company has identified as a strategic growth area. 

• 💰 GCPL subscribed to 1.61 crore equity shares at a total consideration of ₹200 crore, representing a primary capital infusion into Godrej Pet Care. 

• 📊 Following the investment, GCPL continues to hold 100% ownership in Godrej Pet Care. The subsidiary generated ₹2.22 crore revenue (₹222.1 lakh) in FY26.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The investment reinforces GCPL's commitment to the high-growth pet care segment and supports the expansion of its new business vertical.)

reports.text:
HCLTECH: PARTNERS WITH TIM BRASIL TO ENABLE SOUTH AMERICA'S FIRST CROSS-PLATFORM eSIM TRANSFER 📱🌎

• 🤝 HCLTech has partnered with TIM Brasil to enable South America's first cross-platform eSIM transfer capability, enhancing the mobile experience for millions of users in Brazil.  

• 📲 Powered by HCLTech's Device Entitlement Gateway (DEG), the solution allows customers to securely transfer eSIM profiles across devices and operating systems without visiting stores or replacing physical SIM cards. 

• 🚀 The solution simplifies device upgrades, accelerates next-generation mobile service adoption, and delivers a seamless, secure and device-agnostic user experience. 

• 🤖 HCLTech said the collaboration leverages its AI-driven telecom platforms to help TIM Brasil build a more agile, AI-powered telecom ecosystem. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The partnership strengthens HCLTech's telecom digital transformation portfolio and reinforces its capabilities in AI-led network and customer experience solutions.)

reports.text:
VENTURA GUARANTY: NCLT APPROVES AMALGAMATION OF STEP-DOWN SUBSIDIARY WITH VENTURA SECURITIES ⚖️🔄

• 🏛️ The NCLT, Mumbai Bench has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of Ventura Allied Services Pvt. Ltd. (step-down subsidiary) with Ventura Securities Ltd. (subsidiary). The order was pronounced on July 21, 2026. 

• 🤝 The merger aims to simplify the group structure, enhance financial strength and flexibility, reduce administrative costs and improve the long-term competitiveness of the combined entity. 

• 📈 Since Ventura Allied Services is a wholly owned subsidiary of Ventura Securities, no consideration or share exchange will be paid. The existing investment will stand cancelled upon the merger. 

• 📋 The scheme received the required approvals from shareholders, creditors and regulators, including SEBI and stock exchanges, before receiving the NCLT's final sanction.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The internal restructuring simplifies the corporate structure, improves operational efficiency and strengthens the group's long-term governance and financial flexibility.)

PRAVEG: APPROVES ₹53.72 CRORE PREFERENTIAL ISSUE TO STRENGTHEN BALANCE SHEET 💰📈

• 🏢 Praveg's Board has approved a preferential issue aggregating ₹53.72 crore, comprising 8,33,700 equity shares and 11,00,000 convertible warrants, subject to shareholders' and regulatory approvals.  

• 💰 The company will issue 8,33,700 equity shares at ₹275 per share to convert an unsecured inter-corporate loan from Jhaveri Credits & Capital Ltd. into equity, reducing outstanding debt by approximately ₹22.93 crore. 

• 📑 Praveg will also issue 11,00,000 convertible warrants at ₹275 each to members of the Promoter & Promoter Group, aggregating approximately ₹30.25 crore. Each warrant will be convertible into one equity share. 

• 🚀 The company said the fund raise will strengthen its capital structure, improve financial flexibility, reduce debt and support long-term expansion in its eco-luxury hospitality business. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The preferential issue improves Praveg's balance sheet through debt reduction while providing fresh growth capital for future expansion.)

Impact
- Strong Positive.
- Blinkit achieved EBITDA profitability.
- Food delivery growth accelerated.
- New businesses scaling rapidly.
- Strong cash position supports expansion.

Key Takeaway
- Eternal delivered an exceptional Q1FY27 led by strong momentum across Zomato, Blinkit and newer businesses. Blinkit's sustained EBITDA improvement, accelerating food delivery growth and a robust cash balance reinforce the company's leadership across food delivery, quick commerce and adjacent consumer platforms.

SONA COMSTAR: FORMS TWO JVs WITH JAPAN'S DENSO FOR EV & HYBRID POWERTRAIN SYSTEMS 🤝⚡

• 🇯🇵 Sona Comstar has signed definitive agreements with DENSO Corporation, Japan to establish two joint ventures in India for the development, design, manufacturing and marketing of electric and hybrid powertrain systems across multiple vehicle segments. 

• 🚗 The first JV will develop high-voltage liquid-cooled traction inverters, traction motors and generators for electric and hybrid passenger and commercial vehicles. DENSO will hold 51% and Sona Comstar 49%. 

• 🛵 The second JV will focus on air-cooled traction inverters, motors, generators and e-Axles for two- and three-wheelers. DENSO will acquire a 49% stake in Sona Comstar's existing EV traction motor and controller business at an enterprise value of ₹1,750 crore, while Sona Comstar will retain 51% and management control. 

• 🚀 The partnership combines DENSO's global electrification technology with Sona Comstar's manufacturing and engineering expertise to accelerate innovation, expand product offerings and strengthen India's EV ecosystem. The transaction is subject to regulatory approvals.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic partnership with DENSO significantly strengthens Sona Comstar's EV technology capabilities, expands its product portfolio and enhances long-term growth opportunities in electric and hybrid mobility.)

Apollo Micro Systems | Indian Navy Make-II Prototype Sanction

Project Highlights
- Received Make-II Prototype Sanction.
- Project for SAVIOR-ASW platform.
- Indigenous semi-submersible autonomous vessel.
- Designed for anti-submarine warfare surveillance.

Strategic Significance
- Entry into autonomous maritime warfare.
- Strengthens indigenous defence capabilities.
- Expands beyond defence electronics.
- Progressing towards complete defence systems.
- Enhances long-term technology portfolio.

Commercial Perspective
- Prototype approval, not production order.
- No immediate revenue impact.
- Successful development may enable future procurement.
- Commercial orders remain subject to Navy approval.

Key Monitorables
- Prototype development progress.
- Indian Navy validation process.
- Conversion into production contracts.
- Execution under Make-II framework.

Impact
- Positive.
- Strategic capability enhancement.
- Strengthens long-term defence positioning.
- Creates future order optionality.

Key Takeaway
- Apollo Micro Systems' Make-II Prototype Sanction for the SAVIOR-ASW project is strategically significant despite having no immediate revenue impact. It marks the company's entry into autonomous maritime defence and reflects its evolution from a defence electronics supplier to a developer of advanced indigenous defence systems. Successful execution and Navy validation could pave the way for future production opportunities.

MARINETRAFFIC DATA SHOWS COMMERCIAL SHIPPING CONTINUING THROUGH BOTH OF THE MIDDLE EAST'S CRITICAL MARITIME CHOKEPOINTS, ALTHOUGH TRAFFIC PATTERNS REFLECT GROWING OPERATIONAL CAUTION. ON 21 JULY, 9 VERIFIED VESSELS TRANSITED THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ, COMPARED WITH 29 CROSSINGS THROUGH BAB EL MANDEB. 

OVER 20 TO 21 JULY, HORMUZ RECORDED 22 CROSSINGS, WITH 16 USING THE IRANIAN UNILATERAL SCHEME, WHILE BAB EL MANDEB HANDLED 73 CROSSINGS. VESSEL BEHAVIOUR IS ALSO SHIFTING, WITH FOUR VERIFIED U TURNS NEAR THE GULF OF ADEN FOLLOWING HEIGHTENED SECURITY THREATS. COMBINED WITH 61 CONFIRMED MARITIME INCIDENTS REPORTED BY THE IMO, THE DATA SUGGESTS THAT GEOPOLITICAL RISK IS INCREASINGLY INFLUENCING ROUTING DECISIONS, EVEN AS COMMERCIAL TRAFFIC CONTINUES.

HARISH TEXTILE: NCLT RESERVES ORDER IN INSOLVENCY PETITION ⚖️🏛️

• ⚠️ The NCLT, Mumbai Bench has reserved its order in the insolvency petition filed by M/s. Kamlesh Corporation against Harish Textile Engineers under Section 9 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), 2016.  

• 🏛️ The Tribunal declined any further adjournment, heard the applicant's submissions and confirmed that the company's reply will be considered before passing the final order.  

• 📅 The company became aware of the NCLT order on July 21, 2026, after it was uploaded on the NCLT website.  

• 📊 Harish Textile stated that there is no material impact on its financials or operations at this stage, and any financial impact will depend on the final outcome of the proceedings. 

🔴 Impact: Negative (The insolvency petition remains pending before the NCLT, creating legal uncertainty until the Tribunal delivers its final order.)

DEEPAK BUILDERS: COMPLETES JALANDHAR CANTT RAILWAY STATION REDEVELOPMENT 🚉🏗️

• 🏗️ Deepak Builders has successfully completed the redevelopment and construction of Jalandhar Cantt. Railway Station for Indian Railways, with the project now ready for inauguration. 

• 🚉 The redeveloped station features modern passenger amenities, upgraded platforms, improved circulation facilities, enhanced accessibility, contemporary architecture and advanced public utility infrastructure, transforming it into a modern transportation hub. 

• 📈 The company said the successful completion strengthens its credentials in the railway and transportation infrastructure sector, reinforces its execution capability for complex public infrastructure projects and enhances its competitiveness for future railway station redevelopment opportunities. 

• 🤝 Management believes the completion of this landmark project will enhance the company's reputation, stakeholder confidence and long-term business prospects. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Successful execution of a marquee Indian Railways project strengthens Deepak Builders' track record, improving its prospects for securing future infrastructure contracts.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
TEJASSVI AAHARAM: COMPLETES 100% ACQUISITION OF FUNK FOODS VIA ₹51.16 CRORE SHARE SWAP 🍽️🤝

• 🏢 Tejassvi Aaharam has completed the acquisition of 100% equity stake in Funk Foods Pvt. Ltd. (FFPL) by allotting 5.12 crore equity shares on a preferential basis through a share swap, with no cash outflow.  

• 💰 The acquisition is valued at ₹51.16 crore, with shares issued at ₹10 per share (at par) in exchange for the entire equity of FFPL. 

• 🍜 Funk Foods is engaged in the development, manufacturing and export of freeze-dried, clean-label food products, including instant chutneys, soup mixes, ready-to-eat meals, spreads, dips and sauces. It reported FY25 turnover of ₹7.97 crore.  

• 📈 The company said the acquisition will strengthen its existing food business, create synergies and benefit stakeholders. The transaction is not a related-party transaction. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition expands Tejassvi Aaharam's product portfolio through a share-swap transaction without cash outflow, supporting long-term growth and business integration.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
SEJAL GLASS: SECURES ₹121 CRORE ARCHITECTURAL GLASS ORDERS IN UAE & INDIA 🏗️🌍

• 🏆 Sejal Glass' UAE subsidiary has secured five architectural glass orders worth AED 27.6 million (≈₹71 crore) for prestigious hospitality, residential and healthcare projects across the UAE, strengthening its presence in the GCC region. 

• 🇮🇳 The company has also bagged architectural glass projects worth approximately ₹50 crore in India, covering commercial, residential and institutional projects. 

• 🪟 The orders involve the supply of high-performance architectural glass solutions, including insulated glass units (IGUs), solar-control glazing and laminated safety glass, with execution scheduled during 2026–2027.  

• 📈 The company stated that these orders are in addition to its regular order inflows, reflecting healthy business momentum and reinforcing its order pipeline. Customer names have not been disclosed due to competitive sensitivity.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The ₹121 crore order wins strengthen Sejal Glass' order book, improve revenue visibility and reinforce its presence in both domestic and international architectural glass markets.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
SUN TV: UK SUBSIDIARY ISSUES BONUS SHARES TO PARENT COMPANY 🇬🇧📺

• 🏢 The Board of SunRisers Leeds Limited (SRL), a wholly owned subsidiary of Sun TV Network (formerly Northern Superchargers Limited), has approved the issue of 99,999 bonus equity shares of GBP 1 face value each to Sun TV Network. 

• 📈 Following the bonus issue, Sun TV Network's holding in SRL will increase to 1,00,000 equity shares of GBP 1 each. 

• 🔄 The transaction is an internal capital restructuring and there will be no change in Sun TV Network's ownership percentage, as SRL will continue to remain a wholly owned subsidiary. 

• 🏏 SunRisers Leeds is the franchise previously known as Northern Superchargers Limited, reflecting the company's branding transition. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The bonus issue is an internal restructuring within a wholly owned subsidiary and does not change Sun TV Network's ownership or have any material financial impact.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
TVS MOTOR: LAUNCHES TVS RAIDER IN EGYPT, EXPANDS NORTH AFRICA PRESENCE 🏍️🌍

• 🌍 TVS Motor has launched the TVS Raider in Egypt, marking a key step in strengthening its footprint in North Africa and expanding its premium commuter motorcycle portfolio in the region. 

• 🏍️ The TVS Raider is powered by a 124.76cc air & oil-cooled 3V engine producing 12.9 PS power and 11.5 Nm torque, featuring a 5-speed gearbox, full digital LCD cluster, LED headlamp, monoshock suspension and USB charger. 

• 🚀 TVS said Egypt is a strategic gateway to North Africa, and the launch is aimed at attracting young urban riders while accelerating the company's international growth strategy. 

• 🛍️ The motorcycle is available through select dealerships in Egypt and will be rolled out across the dealer network by July 2026 in Black and Yellow colour options. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The Egypt launch strengthens TVS Motor's international expansion strategy, enhances its presence in North Africa and supports long-term export growth.)

Jefferies in IndiaMart
U-P, TP Rs 1650

Q1 revs were in line while margins/profits were ahead.

Decline in paid supplier base for third straight quarter was a key negative surprise.

Margin expansion was driven by lower customer acquisition costs, which should normalize once the gross addition picks up.

Continued weakness in sub adds can disrupt network effects historically enjoyed by the platform

Cut FY28-29E EPS by ~1.5-4%

Nomura on IndiaMart
Reduce, TP Rs 1810

Paying subscriber addition woes continue – reduction continued in 1Q

Product evolution critical to improve subscriber addition in medium to long term

Believe subscriber addition has to turn meaningfully positive for stock to rise significantly from here

Value Indiamart’s core business at 18x P/E and continue to assign a 1x book value to its recent investments.

**Nykaa | Brokerage Update (HSBC)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy (Upgraded).
- Target Price: ₹380.

**Investment Thesis**
- Sees Nykaa as online BPC leader.
- Gaining market share in fashion.
- Strong long-term growth visibility.

**Earnings Outlook**
- Raises FY28 EBITDA estimate by 12%.
- Driven by stronger BPC margins.
- Fashion business growth supports earnings.

**Long-Term Outlook**
- Growth visibility remains strong beyond FY30.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC upgraded Nykaa to Buy, citing its leadership in online beauty & personal care, increasing fashion market share, improving profitability, and strong long-term growth prospects extending beyond FY30.**

**Bajaj Auto | Brokerage Update (MOSL)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy (Upgraded).
- Target Price: ₹12,096.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Earnings beat estimates.
- Strong margins drove outperformance.
- Outlook remains positive across segments.

**Management Commentary**
- Targets ~2.50 lakh monthly exports from Q2.
- Export volume ramp-up expected.

**Cash Flow**
- FCF reached ₹2,300 Cr in Q1.
- FCF doubled YoY.
- Cash conversion at ~80% of PAT.

**Key Takeaway**
- **MOSL upgraded Bajaj Auto to Buy, citing strong margin-led earnings, robust free cash flow generation, improving export outlook, and positive growth prospects across business segments.**

**Indian Hotels | Brokerage Update (Morgan Stanley)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Equal-weight.
- Target Price: ₹783.

**Business Outlook**
- Q1 growth momentum expected in Q2.
- FY27 outlook remains firm.
- Standalone RevPAR met expectations.

**Guidance**
- Maintains double-digit FY27 revenue growth guidance.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley remains constructive on Indian Hotels, expecting growth momentum to continue into Q2 with a firm FY27 outlook, supported by stable RevPAR performance and management's confidence in delivering double-digit revenue growth.**

**TVS Motor | Brokerage Update (Macquarie)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Outperform.
- Target Price: ₹4,325.

**Q1 Assessment**
- PAT beat estimates.
- Lower credit costs supported earnings.
- Margins remained resilient.
- Asset quality improved.

**Key Monitor**
- Sustainability of current performance.
- Margin and asset quality trends.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie remains positive on TVS Motor, citing better-than-expected Q1 earnings, resilient margins, and stronger asset quality, while highlighting the sustainability of these improvements as the key factor to watch going forward.**

**Bandhan Bank | Brokerage Update (Macquarie)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underperform.
- Target Price: ₹130.

**Q1 Assessment**
- PAT missed estimates.
- Margin outlook lowered.
- Credit costs remain elevated.

**Outlook**
- Recovery outlook remains uncertain.
- Earnings visibility remains weak.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie remains cautious on Bandhan Bank, citing weaker-than-expected earnings, pressure on margins, elevated credit costs, and an uncertain recovery trajectory, leading it to maintain an Underperform rating with a ₹130 target price.**

**M&M Financial Services | Morgan Stanley View**

**Brokerage View**
- **Equal-weight** maintained.
- Target price raised to **₹370**.
- Earlier target price **₹335**.
- Good Q1 performance.

**Q1 Highlights**
- PAT beat estimates by **7%**.
- Lower operating costs aided earnings.
- Lower credit costs supported profitability.
- Slightly higher NIM assumptions.

**Estimate Revisions**
- FY27–FY29 EPS raised by **8%**.
- Reflects improved cost outlook.
- Credit cost assumptions lowered.
- Margins expected to remain healthy.

**Valuation View**
- Higher target price assigned.
- Macro risks remain a consideration.
- Historical volatility acknowledged.
- Scope for valuation re-rating.

**Impact**
- **Positive**.
- Earnings outlook improved.
- Better profitability expectations.
- Valuation upside remains.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley maintained its Equal-weight rating on M&M Financial Services while raising the target price to ₹370. A stronger-than-expected Q1, driven by lower operating and credit costs, prompted higher earnings estimates, with further re-rating potential despite macro and cyclical risks.**

reports.text:
India paid an additional ₹2.02 trillion for crude oil imports in the AMJ 2026 quarter.

This was higher by 77%, despite volume dropping by 4.5%.

Axis has come up with a Buy report on 
Steel Strip Wheels (SSWL) with a target price of 305.

However, 2 things to note - 
1> Their estimates are petty conservative, management guidance is actually higher with a positive bias. So, EPS could very well be 10-15% higher. 

2> They have valued the company at 14 PE.
I am not sure why. If the company cracks the export market and is able to grow at 20%+ with margin upside, I am not sure if it will trade at such low valuations.

SO, the bigger opportunity here is the valuation rerating.
If market decides that it deserves a 25-30 PE which is still lower than most auto ancillary stocks, then this can be a huge winner. Lets see how this goes.
SUDARSHAN CHEMICAL

reports.text:
Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on Jindal Stainless 
BUY rating and TP at ₹1,000 

Key reasons behind the bullish stance:
✅ Best placed to benefit from rising domestic stainless steel consumption
✅ Higher share of value-added products to support profitability
✅ Strong balance sheet provides headroom to nearly double capacity from the current 4.2 MTPA over the medium term
✅ Attractive valuations relative to expected RoE

A side note: Amit Dixit the author of the report has been constructive on Jindal Stainless since much lower price levels during his time at previous brokerage houses.

**Paytm | CLSA View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- EBITDA reported at **₹200 crore**.
- EBITDA marginally beat estimates.
- Payments GMV growth accelerated to **31% YoY**.
- Financial services revenue growth strengthened.

**Growth Drivers**
- Merchant payments gaining momentum.
- Financial services scaling well.
- Stable contribution margin at **55%**.
- Lower fixed costs boosted profitability.

**Key Monitorables**
- Payment take rate declined sequentially.
- Lower doubtful debt provisions aided EBITDA.
- Wallet license application submitted.
- Higher operating expenses expected ahead.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** **Outperform**.
- Target Price **₹1,050**.
- FY27–FY29 EBITDA estimates cut **2–3%**.
- Limited upside after recent stock rally.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA acknowledges Paytm's strong operational momentum in payments and financial services, but believes much of the optimism around a possible UPI MDR return is already reflected in the stock price. While profitability continues to improve, higher operating expenses could moderate earnings growth.**

**Paytm | Citi View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Revenue grew **28% YoY**.
- EBITDA reached **₹200 crore**.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **16%**.
- Contribution profit at **55%** of revenue.

**Growth Drivers**
- Higher merchant loan distribution.
- Lower cloud infrastructure costs.
- Merchant ecosystem expanding.
- Operating leverage improving.

**Key Monitorables**
- Payment margins impacted by device rentals.
- Potential UPI MDR implementation.
- MDR could improve payment margins.
- Earnings estimates revised upward.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** **Buy**.
- Target Price raised to **₹1,560**.
- Estimates increased after Q1.
- FY28 EBITDA could rise **8–10%** if UPI MDR is introduced.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains bullish on Paytm, supported by strong operating performance, improving cost efficiency and robust merchant lending growth. A potential introduction of UPI MDR on large merchants could provide an additional boost to payment margins and future earnings.**

**Paytm | Goldman Sachs View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong quarterly performance.
- Revenue growth accelerated.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **20%**.
- Bonus issue plans deferred.

**Growth Drivers**
- Offline payment market share gains.
- Online merchant additions accelerated.
- Merchant loan distribution remained strong.
- Consumer postpaid business expanding.

**Business Outlook**
- Payment ecosystem gaining momentum.
- Merchant acquisition continues.
- Consumer lending supports growth.
- Profitability expected to improve sharply.

**Brokerage View**
- **Goldman Sachs:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹1,500**.
- FY27 revenue growth estimated at **27% YoY**.
- FY27 EBITDA expected to more than double.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Goldman Sachs remains positive on Paytm, driven by accelerating payments growth, sustained merchant lending traction and expanding consumer financial products. The brokerage expects strong revenue momentum and a sharp improvement in profitability through FY27.**

**UltraTech Cement | Jefferies View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong execution continued.
- EBITDA grew **14% YoY**.
- Double-digit volume growth.
- Better pricing supported earnings.

**Growth Drivers**
- Healthy volume momentum.
- Stable pricing outlook.
- Strong operational execution.
- Capacity expansion progressing.

**Management Outlook**
- Pricing expected stable through monsoon.
- Peak industry costs anticipated.
- Double-digit volume growth expected in FY27.
- EBITDA/tonne target **₹1,400** by **Q4FY28**.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹14,065**.
- Positive on earnings trajectory.
- Confident on margin expansion.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains bullish on UltraTech Cement, citing superior execution, sustained double-digit volume growth, stable pricing and continued margin improvement. The brokerage expects the company to achieve ₹1,400 EBITDA per tonne by Q4FY28, supported by strong operational discipline and industry-leading scale.**

**UltraTech Cement | JPMorgan View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- PAT reported at **₹2,600 crore**.
- EBITDA/tonne at **₹1,214**.
- Volumes exceeded expectations.
- Quarter delivered a slight beat.

**Growth Drivers**
- Higher domestic volumes.
- Scale advantage remains strong.
- ICEM profitability improving.
- Market leadership intact.

**Key Monitorables**
- QoQ costs increased.
- Price hikes offset by costs.
- Q2 may remain challenging.
- Monsoon could impact demand.

**Management Outlook**
- ICEM EBITDA/tonne target **₹1,000**.
- Scale to support resilience.
- Better positioned than peers.
- Long-term outlook remains positive.

**Brokerage View**
- **JPMorgan:** **Overweight**.
- Target Price **₹13,300**.
- Positive on execution.
- Scale remains competitive advantage.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains constructive on UltraTech Cement, citing stronger-than-expected volume growth, improving ICEM profitability and unmatched scale, which should help the company outperform peers despite near-term cost pressures and seasonal demand challenges.**

**UltraTech Cement | Citi View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Consolidated EBITDA grew **14% YoY**.
- India volumes increased **13% YoY**.
- Realizations improved **1% YoY**.
- India EBITDA/tonne at **₹1,208**.

**Growth Drivers**
- Strong domestic volume growth.
- Realizations rose **~4% QoQ**.
- Cost management remained effective.
- ICL profitability improved significantly.

**Management Outlook**
- EBITDA/tonne target **₹1,400** by **Q4FY28E**.
- Q2 costs to rise **₹130–140/tonne**.
- Industry expects stable pricing.
- India expansion progressing as planned.

**Capital Allocation**
- Growth investments remain priority.
- Dividend policy unchanged.
- Net Debt/EBITDA expected below **1x**.
- Next expansion phase to be announced.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹14,750**.
- Positive on growth and cost control.
- Attractive valuation versus historical peak.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains bullish on UltraTech Cement, driven by strong volume growth, disciplined cost management, improving profitability and a robust expansion pipeline. Healthy balance sheet strength and continued capacity additions support sustained long-term earnings growth.**

**UltraTech Cement | CLSA View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong quarter across metrics.
- Volume grew **13% YoY**.
- EBITDA per tonne **₹1,183**.
- Performance beat industry trends.

**Growth Drivers**
- Better-than-expected cost control.
- Robust execution continued.
- Capacity expansion on track.
- Margin accretion expected.

**Management Outlook**
- Q2 cost increase guided at **₹130–140/tonne**.
- Lower than normal seasonal inflation.
- Earnings outlook remains constructive.
- Strong free cash flow expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** **High Conviction Outperform**.
- Target Price **₹14,000**.
- Mid-teen earnings CAGR expected over **FY26–30**.
- Improving ROCE supports valuation.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA remains highly positive on UltraTech Cement, citing strong execution, superior cost control, ongoing capacity expansion and improving margins, which are expected to drive mid-teen earnings growth, higher ROCE and robust free cash flow over the medium term.**

**CLSA | India FMCG Price Survey**

**Key Findings**
- Price hikes outpaced cuts **5x**.

- Inflation remained category-specific.
- Consumer pricing stayed volatile.

- Home care recorded biggest hikes.
- Personal care prices stayed volatile.
- Food inflation remained selective.
- Mixed pricing across FMCG categories.

**Product Highlights**
- Britannia biscuits up **8%**.
- Coconut hair oil down **11%**.
- VAHO prices up **14%**.
- Pricing trends remained divergent.

**Sector Outlook**
- Home care pricing remains firm.
- Personal care volatility expected.
- Commodity movements remain key.
- FMCG pricing actions likely continue.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA's latest FMCG survey indicates pricing power is returning, with price increases significantly outnumbering cuts. Home and personal care remain the most volatile categories, while selective price hikes should support revenue growth for FMCG companies.**

reports.text: It was a PEAD stock around 900 rs couple of quarters back

ACCIDENT OF THE DAY : WAAREE, PREMIER ENERGIES 

*ALMM-II extension, -ve for Solar mfg, +ve for developers* 

 *What’s new?* 
- MNRE, on Saturday, announced limited extension to the implementation of ALMM List-II (Approved List of Models & Manufacturers for solar cells) for Net Metering and Open Access (OA) solar projects to Dec-26 (earlier: May-26). 
- The decision followed by industry concerns that domestic cell manufacturing capacity remains insufficient to meet near-term demand, potentially delaying projects and increasing costs.
- MNRE stated that the move is intended to facilitate a smoother transition towards domestic cell sourcing while protecting investments already made by developers and module manufacturers.
- Interestingly, the order specifically notes that the extension will also help module manufacturers monetize existing inventories and provide time for the domestic solar cell industry to scale up capacity before the full implementation of ALMM List-II.

 *Our view* 
 
1) Impact on Solar Module Manufacturers (Waaree Energies & Premier Energies)
Near-term impact: Negative
- This extension reduces the value creation opportunity for the manufacturers such as Waaree Energies and Premier Energies with stronger backward intergration
- As the industry manufacturing capacity expands and demand supply mismatch reduces over the next six months, the pricing power for early movers of cell capacity additon would erode for the DCR based modules
- On the non DCR module, there can be an increase in demand in Q2 and Q3, however, given the lower realization and margin compared to DCR modules, this would still be net negative on the FY27 financials of these companies.

2) *Impact on CleanMax* 
 *Near term impact: Positive* 
- For RE open access and rooftop developers like CleanMax, this could have a significant boost in the segment as there can be an increased demand from consumer to benefit from the capex cost savings
- For C&I developer, execution certainty is often more valuable than marginal equipment localization benefits, making this policy amendment an incremental positive for growth and project returns over the next six months.

 *3) Industry impact* 
- In our view, the strong solar capacity addition in FY26 was partly driven by capacity additions ramp up to benefit prior to the change in regulations.
- We earlier, therefore had anticipated the spurt of FY26 (44.6GW addition) to subside marginally in FY27 and estimated a 35GW addition for the year.
- However, the announced extension, we see upside risk to our estimate. BROKERAGE NOTE

**Goldman Sachs | Auto Ancillary Sector Initiation**

**Sector View**
- Product mix transformation underway.
- Diversifying beyond auto cycles.
- Precision machining gaining traction.
- Higher-value businesses emerging.

**Industry Outlook**
- Revenue growth: **7%****/12****%****/10****%** (FY27E/28E/29E).
- EBITDA growth: **7%****/26****%****/14****%** (FY27E/28E/29E).
- Structural growth remains strong.
- Margin profile expected to improve.

**Top Picks**
- **Sansera Engineering:** **Buy**, TP **₹4,130**.
- **Craftsman Automation:** **Buy**, TP **₹11,600**.
- **Samvardhana Motherson:** **Neutral**, TP **₹148**.
- **Bharat Forge:** **Neutral**, TP **₹2,120**.

**Investment Thesis**
- Market views sector as cyclical.
- Pricing power seen improving.
- Manufacturing capabilities expanding.
- Transformation supports re-rating.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Goldman Sachs believes leading auto ancillary companies are evolving into diversified precision manufacturing businesses, reducing cyclicality and creating a stronger long-term earnings growth opportunity.**

**Tata Technologies | Q1FY27 JPMorgan Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Revenue beat estimates.
- Margins missed expectations.
- Strong quarterly execution.
- Deal ramp-ups supported growth.

**Business Performance**
- Revenue grew **4.3% QoQ (CC)**.
- Services grew **4.3% QoQ (CC)**.
- Auto segment grew **4.3% QoQ (CC)**.
- Growth driven by deal ramp-ups.

**Growth Outlook**
- FY27 double-digit organic CC growth reiterated.
- Deal pipeline remains strong.
- German market faces headwinds.
- Client restructuring impacting demand.

**Brokerage View**
- **JPMorgan:** Underweight, TP **₹540**.
- Stock up **28%** since May.
- Valuation rich at **38x/33x FY27/28E P/E**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains cautious on Tata Technologies despite strong revenue growth and management's confident outlook, citing German market headwinds and expensive valuations after the recent stock rally.**

**Oberoi Realty | JPMorgan Update**

**Business Performance**
- Commercial business resilient.
- Residential outlook strong.
- Pre-sales missed estimates.
- Down **21%** vs estimates.

**Financial Highlights**
- Negative free cash flow.
- Working capital driven.
- Funding costs increased QoQ.

**Growth Outlook**
- FY27/28 pre-sales may rise.
- Residential to drive growth.
- Sky City mall occupancy improved.
- Higher annuity NOI expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **JPMorgan:** Overweight, TP **₹2,050**.
- Positive long-term outlook.
- Commercial remains resilient.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains positive on Oberoi Realty, expecting stronger residential sales next quarter and higher recurring income from improved Sky City mall occupancy despite weaker Q1 pre-sales and temporary cash flow pressure.**

**CEAT | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA missed estimates.
- Margins remained under pressure.
- Near-term profitability subdued.

**Growth Outlook**
- Price hikes to aid margins.
- Recovery expected in 2HFY27.
- Premium segment focus.
- Export growth continues.

**Valuation**
- Trading at **7.1x FY28 EV/EBITDA**.
- Valuation seen attractive.
- EBITDA CAGR **16%** (FY25-29).

**Brokerage View**
- **Nomura:** Buy, TP **₹4,266**.
- Positive long-term outlook.
- Margin recovery expected.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains bullish on CEAT, expecting price hikes, premiumisation and export growth to drive margin recovery and a 16% EBITDA CAGR despite a weak Q1FY27.**

**CEAT | Q1FY27 Kotak Institutional Equities Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA **₹365 crore**.
- Down **6% YoY**.
- Down **38% QoQ**.
- Missed estimates by **13%**.

**Business Performance**
- India business remained weak.
- Camso performance disappointed.
- Domestic execution stayed healthy.
- Acquisition risks persist.

**Outlook**
- Weak profitability in Q2.
- Raw material headwinds continue.
- Currency pressures remain.
- Recovery expected in 2HFY27.

**Brokerage View**
- **Kotak Institutional Equities:** Reduce, TP **₹3,350**.
- Cautious near-term outlook.
- Camso remains key risk.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kotak remains cautious on CEAT as weaker-than-expected profitability, raw material inflation and Camso integration challenges are likely to weigh on earnings before an expected recovery in 2HFY27.**

**Adani Green Energy | Macquarie Update**

**Growth Outlook**
- BESS added to estimates.
- **10 GWh** annual additions.
- EBITDA upside expected.
- Execution remains strong.

**Capacity Expansion**
- Commissioned **5 GW+** in FY26.
- Operational capacity **20 GW+**.
- **5.5 GW/year** additions till FY30.
- Khavda targets **30 GW**.

**Earnings Outlook**
- Base-case EBITDA CAGR **30%**.
- Bull-case CAGR **40%**.
- FY28/29 EBITDA raised **6%****/10****%**.
- EPS impacted by higher interest.

**Brokerage View**
- **Macquarie:** Outperform, TP **₹1,800** *(raised)*.
- Positive on BESS.
- Long-term growth intact.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie remains highly bullish on Adani Green, citing rapid renewable capacity additions, BESS integration and the Khavda mega project as key drivers of 30–40% EBITDA growth over the next five years.**

**Kotak Mahindra Bank | Q1FY27 Jefferies Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Profit **₹4,100 crore**.
- Up **26% YoY**.
- Beat estimates.
- Better NII and fees.

**Business Performance**
- Loans grew **15%**.
- NII up **9%**.
- Deposits grew **12%**.
- LDR improved to **89%**.

**Asset Quality**
- Credit quality improved.
- MFI trends strengthened.
- Credit cards improved.
- Credit cost **0.5%**.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹460**.
- FY27 EPS raised **2%**.
- CEO succession key monitorable.
- Valuation remains reasonable.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on Kotak Mahindra Bank, supported by improving credit quality, healthy earnings growth and better fee income, while CEO succession remains the key near-term monitorable.**

**Kotak Mahindra Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

**Business Performance**
- Steady quarterly performance.
- Broad-based loan growth.
- Asset quality remained stable.
- Loan growth matched peers.

**Financial Highlights**
- NIM declined sequentially.
- LDR improved.
- RoA remained above **2%**.
- Cost discipline supported earnings.

**Key Monitorables**
- Deposit growth lagged peers.
- Capital levels increased.
- RoE remained constrained.
- Efficiency remains a focus.

**Brokerage View**
- **Bernstein:** Market Perform, TP **₹500**.
- Stable fundamentals.
- Growth trails larger peers.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein views Kotak Mahindra Bank as fundamentally stable with healthy profitability and asset quality, but believes slower deposit growth, margin pressure and elevated capital levels continue to constrain returns versus larger private sector peers.**

**Axis Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

**Business Performance**
- Mixed quarterly performance.
- Corporate-led loan growth.
- Balance sheet remained strong.
- NII growth improved.

**Financial Highlights**
- NIM down **16 bps QoQ**.
- Margin compression persisted.
- Other income remained weak.
- Profit up **23% YoY**.

**Asset Quality**
- Credit costs declined YoY.
- Cost discipline continued.
- Asset quality softened marginally.

**Brokerage View**
- **Bernstein:** Outperform, TP **₹1,600**.
- Positive on growth.
- Margins remain key monitorable.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein remains constructive on Axis Bank, with strong corporate-led growth and disciplined costs supporting earnings, though margin compression and a slight deterioration in asset quality remain watchpoints.**

**Axis Bank | Q1FY27 Jefferies Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Profit **₹7,100 crore**.
- Up **23% YoY**.
- Beat estimates.
- Lower costs supported earnings.

**Business Performance**
- NII grew **8%**.
- Loan growth **19% YoY**.
- NIM down **16 bps QoQ**.
- Corporate mix impacted margins.

**Asset Quality**
- Asset quality remained strong.
- Credit cost **0.7%**.
- Retail deposits improving.
- Fee income recovery expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,700**.
- FY27 EPS raised **2%**.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on Axis Bank, supported by strong loan growth, healthy asset quality and improving retail deposits, with margin pressure expected to ease in 2HFY27.**

reports.text:
**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Jefferies Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Profit **₹19,100 crore**.
- Up **5% YoY**.
- Results in line.
- Lower costs supported earnings.

**Business Performance**
- Loan growth **16% YoY**.
- Corporate lending increased.
- NIM down **12 bps QoQ**.
- NII growth **7%**.

**Profitability**
- Opex growth moderated.
- Credit costs declined.
- Low slippages supported profits.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,050**.
- PBT CAGR seen at **15%**.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains constructive on HDFC Bank, with strong loan growth, disciplined costs and lower credit costs supporting earnings despite continued margin compression from higher corporate lending.**

**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Results in line.
- Margins declined QoQ.
- PAT met estimates.
- Credit costs stable.

**Business Performance**
- Loan growth accelerated.
- Deposit growth improved.
- Asset quality remained pristine.
- Growth momentum strengthened.

**Outlook**
- FCNR-led growth expected.
- Normalization continues.
- Valuation remains attractive.

**Brokerage View**
- **Nomura:** Buy, TP **₹950**.
- Positive on growth.
- Asset quality a key strength.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains positive on HDFC Bank, expecting improving loan and deposit growth, pristine asset quality and FCNR-led acceleration despite near-term margin compression.**

**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

**Business Performance**
- Steady quarterly performance.
- Healthy balance-sheet growth.
- Asset quality remained resilient.
- Franchise normalization continued.

**Financial Highlights**
- Margin pressure persisted.
- Loan yields softened.
- Funding costs increased.
- NII growth remained constrained.

**Profitability**
- Operating efficiency supported.
- Stable asset quality cushioned.
- Profitability remained resilient.

**Brokerage View**
- **Bernstein:** Outperform, TP **₹1,150**.
- Positive on franchise quality.
- Margin headwinds persist.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein remains positive on HDFC Bank, citing healthy balance-sheet growth, resilient asset quality and strong operating efficiency, while noting that margin pressure continues to limit near-term earnings growth.**

**ICICI Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

**Business Performance**
- Strongest loan growth.
- Deposit momentum improved.
- Industry-leading NIM.
- Profitability remained strong.

**Financial Highlights**
- Growth outpaced peers.
- Margins stayed resilient.
- RoA reached **2.5%**.
- Earnings quality remained high.

**Brokerage View**
- **Bernstein:** Market Perform, TP **₹1,550**.
- Best-performing large private bank.
- Balanced valuation view.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein views ICICI Bank as the best-performing large private bank this quarter, driven by superior loan growth, strong deposit traction, industry-leading margins and a sector-leading 2.5% RoA, while maintaining a Market Perform rating on valuation.**

**ICICI Bank | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- PAT beat estimates **6%**.
- Margins remained resilient.
- Strong earnings quality.
- Outperformed expectations.

**Business Performance**
- Sector-leading loan growth.
- Loan growth re-accelerated.
- Asset quality remained pristine.
- Margin resilience continued.

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27-28 EPS raised **5%**.
- Growth outlook strengthened.
- Valuation remains reasonable.

**Brokerage View**
- **Nomura:** Buy, TP **₹1,700**.
- Positive on margins.
- Favors strong growth outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains bullish on ICICI Bank, citing sector-leading loan growth, resilient margins, pristine asset quality and a quality earnings beat despite a challenging margin environment.**

**Private Banks | Q1FY27 Morgan Stanley Update**

**ICICI Bank**
- Strongest loan growth.
- NIM expanded.
- Fee income remained strong.
- Best operating performance.

**HDFC Bank**
- NII missed estimates.
- Higher NIM compression.
- Margins remained under pressure.

**Axis Bank**
- NII missed estimates.
- NIM compressed further.
- Corporate mix reduced spreads.

**Kotak Mahindra Bank**
- NIM met estimates.
- Results beat forecasts.
- Overall performance solid.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley prefers ICICI Bank for its superior loan growth and margin expansion, while Kotak delivered a positive surprise. HDFC Bank and Axis Bank faced pressure from higher-than-expected NIM compression.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Citi Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA **₹9,370 crore**.
- Up **32% YoY**.
- Beat estimates by **14%**.
- Higher realizations drove growth.
- India EBITDA **₹15,110/ton**.

**Operational Highlights**
- Domestic volumes up **~1% YoY**.
- Weak retail and longs.
- Q2 volumes expected higher.
- Auto price hikes reflected.

**Cost & Margin Outlook**
- Q2 prices may soften.
- Coking coal costs peaked.
- Lower ore costs expected.
- Better operating leverage ahead.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net Debt/EBITDA **1.5x**.
- Comfortable below **2.5x**.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** Sell, TP **₹1,100**.
- Sees EBITDA peak in Q1.
- Valuation remains expensive.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Despite a strong Q1 driven by higher realizations and improved margins, Citi remains cautious, expecting softer pricing after Q1 and believing EBITDA has likely peaked for the near term.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Jefferies Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA up **38% YoY**.
- Beat estimates by **20%**.
- Fifth straight double-digit growth.
- Strong operating momentum.

**Earnings Outlook**
- FY27-28 EPS raised **1-4%**.
- EPS may treble FY26-29.
- FY27 EBITDA seen improving.
- Profitability outlook strong.

**Growth Outlook**
- Capacity target **80 MT** by FY32.
- Potential top-3 global producer.
- Expansion remains on track.
- Long-term growth intact.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,650**.
- Top India Metals pick.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains highly bullish on JSW Steel, citing strong earnings momentum, rising profitability and an aggressive capacity expansion plan that could make it one of the world's top three steel producers by FY32.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Goldman Sachs Update**

**Operational Highlights**
- India sales **6.3 MT**.
- Volumes up **5.5% YoY**.
- Growth despite BF-3 outage.
- Retail demand remained weak.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net debt down **₹7,700 crore QoQ**.
- BPSL divestiture completed.
- Net Debt/EBITDA **1.46x**.
- Well below **2.5x** threshold.

**Growth Outlook**
- BF-3 furnace commissioned.
- Higher Q2 volumes guided.
- Expansion capex on track.
- Downstream projects support margins.
- H2FY27 volume recovery expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **Goldman Sachs:** Buy, TP **₹1,525**.
- Positive despite softer Q2 spreads.
- Volume growth to offset pressure.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Goldman Sachs remains bullish on JSW Steel, supported by strong deleveraging, higher production after BF-3 commissioning and capacity expansion, with H2FY27 volume growth expected to offset softer near-term spreads.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Morgan Stanley Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA beat expectations.
- Better realizations supported.
- Strong cost control.
- Volumes remained softer.

**Balance Sheet**
- BPSL proceeds received.
- Further deleveraging achieved.
- Financial position strengthened.

**Outlook**
- Q2FY27 seasonally weak.
- Near-term softness expected.
- Long-term outlook intact.

**Brokerage View**
- **Morgan Stanley:** Overweight, TP **₹1,470**.
- EBITDA exceeded estimates.
- Positive beyond seasonality.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley remains positive on JSW Steel as better realizations, disciplined cost control and continued deleveraging outweighed weaker volumes, with investors advised to look beyond the seasonally soft Q2.**

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 JPMorgan Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA grew **32% YoY**.
- India business remained strong.
- US Ohio performed well.
- Italy operations stayed robust.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net Debt/EBITDA **~1.5x**.
- Down from **3.2x YoY**.
- Target below **2.5x**.
- Capex remains on track.

**Outlook**
- Spreads may moderate in Q2.
- Raw material costs peaked.
- Recovery expected in 2HFY27.
- Margin outlook constructive.

**Brokerage View**
- **JPMorgan:** Overweight, TP **₹1,500** *(raised)*.
- Top pick in India Metals.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains highly positive on JSW Steel, citing strong global operations, a much stronger balance sheet and expectations of margin recovery in 2HFY27 despite near-term spread moderation.**

**Reliance Industries | Brokerage Update**

**Brokerage Ratings**
- Goldman Sachs – Buy | TP ₹1,870.
- CLSA – Outperform | TP ₹1,800.
- Nomura – Buy | TP ₹1,690.
- Motilal Oswal – Buy | TP ₹1,550.
- Macquarie – Outperform | TP ₹1,510.
- Morgan Stanley – Q1 earnings beat estimates.

**Key Positives**
- Strong O2C and energy performance.
- Jio delivered steady growth.
- Retail recovery remains a key trigger.
- New Energy ramp-up in focus.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Brokerages remain positive on Reliance Industries after a Q1FY27 earnings beat, supported by strong O2C, resilient Jio performance and improving retail and New Energy outlook.**

- Higher realizations supported earnings.
- India EBITDA **₹15,110/ton**.
- Margins up **23.3% QoQ**.

- Margins may soften in Q2.
- Seasonal pressure on longs.
- Coal costs remain elevated.

- Capacity target **49 MTPA** by FY30.
- Expansion pipeline remains strong.
- Margin-accretive projects underway.

- **Kotak Institutional Equities:** **Upgrade to Buy**, TP **₹1,450**.
- Recent correction attractive.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Jio Business**
- Results broadly in line.

- Telecom outlook remains stable.

**Growth Triggers**
- Retail recovery.
- FMCG scale-up.
- New Energy expansion.
- AI & Datacentre growth.

**Brokerage View**

- Positive long-term outlook.
- Multiple growth catalysts.

**Key Takeaway**
- **MOSL remains constructive on Reliance, expecting recovery in Retail, resilient Jio performance and scaling of FMCG, New Energy, AI and Datacentre businesses to drive long-term value creation.**

Brokerage firm Ashika remains bullish on the banking sector.

Its top picks are:

- Axis Bank – ₹1,663
- City Union Bank – ₹280
-Karur Vysya Bank – ₹373

Dark horse picks:

- Bandhan Bank – ₹271
- Bank of Baroda – ₹316
